content_hints.description: Trollskull Alley is filled with people who can shape the characters’ day-to-day lives in Waterdeep. Buildings not specifically identified on the map are rowhouses that serve as private residences for upper-middle class Waterdavians who can afford housekeepers, groundskeepers, and nannies.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see Trollskull Alley, a bustling thoroughfare where the characters can shape their day-to-day lives in Waterdeep. Rowhouses line the streets, serving as private residences for upper-middle-class Waterdavians.
event_triggers: On entry: Lif slams a door shut behind the party, then slowly opens it again -- testing their nerve.
On renovation complete: Lif begins serving drinks to patrons, glasses floating through the air.

content_hints.description: Trollskull Alley is filled with people who can shape the characters’ day-to-day lives in Waterdeep, where the locations described below are keyed to the map. Buildings not specifically identified on the map are rowhouses that serve as private residences for upper-middle class Waterdavians who can afford housekeepers, groundskeepers, and nannies.
content_hints.read_aloud: You step into Trollskull Alley, a narrow alley filled with people who shape the characters’ day-to-day lives in Waterdeep. The air is thick with the sounds and smells of the city, and you see rowhouses serving as private residences for the upper-middle class.

npcs.name: Tarsar
npcs.role: wizened ex-adventurer who leans on a cane

npcs.name: Thort
npcs.role: a wizened ex-adventurer who lives in the small shop

content_hints.description: Trollskull Alley is a place where the sounds of metalworking mingle with the bustle of the city. Here, you can find shops selling wooden weapons and shields, alongside the smoke and steam billowing from an indoor forge where metal weapons, armor, and tools are made by the fire genasi Embric and the water genasi Avi.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see the grand, four-story Trollskull Manor, boasting balconies, a turret, and five chimneys, standing as one of the grandest buildings in Trollskull Alley.

npcs.name: Avi
npcs.dialogue: Embric does the shaping, I do the finishing. He is fire, I am patience. Between us, we make things that last.
npcs.notes: Water genasi. Quiet, precise. Handles the business side. Married to Embric.

npcs.name: Embric
npcs.dialogue: You want sharp? I give you sharp. You want beautiful? That costs extra. You want both? Now we are talking.
npcs.notes: Fire genasi. Passionate, dramatic. Forges weapons and armor. Married to Avi.

content_hints.description: Trollskull Alley is filled with people who can shape the characters’ day-to-day lives in Waterdeep, and the locations described below are keyed to the map of Trollskull Alley. Buildings not specifically identified on the map are rowhouses that serve as private residences for upper-middle class Waterdavians who can afford housekeepers, groundskeepers, and nannies.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see Trollskull Alley, a place teeming with people who can shape your day-to-day lives in Waterdeep. The air is thick with the smells of commerce, and you notice buildings lining the narrow streets, hinting at the diverse trades and activities that take place here.

npcs.name: Fala Lefaliir
npcs.dialogue: This one is for headaches. This one is for heartbreak. No, I am not joking -- grief sits in the body like a stone, and willowbark alone will not shift it.
npcs.notes: Non-binary wood elf herbalist. Gentle, perceptive. Sells potions and herbal remedies. Member of the Emerald Enclave. Knows local gossip through their customers' ailments.

content_hints.description: Trollskull Alley is a place where people shape the characters’ day-to-day lives in Waterdeep, and they will likely return to it many times during the adventure to get to know their neighbors. Buildings not specifically identified on the map are rowhouses that serve as private residences for upper-middle class Waterdavians who can afford housekeepers, groundskeepers, and nannies.
content_hints.read_aloud: You find yourselves in Trollskull Alley, a place teeming with the daily lives of Waterdeep's inhabitants. The air hums with the bustle of the city, and you can sense the history and the potential for new connections in this neighborhood.

npcs.name: Laiba “Nana” Rosse
npcs.role: caretaker and private tutor for Terenzio and Elzerina

event_triggers: On quest accept: Vincent provides a dossier on the target with unsettling accuracy.

content_hints.description: Trollskull Alley is filled with people who can shape the characters’ day-to-day lives in Waterdeep, where they will likely return many times and get to know their neighbors as time passes. Buildings not specifically identified on the map are rowhouses that serve as private residences for upper-middle class Waterdavians who can afford housekeepers, groundskeepers, and nannies.
content_hints.read_aloud: You find yourselves in Trollskull Alley, a narrow passage filled with the daily lives of Waterdeep's inhabitants. You see the cobblestones slick beneath your boots, and a gutter runs down the center, carrying dark water and the occasional scurrying shape. Beyond the alley's mouth, the noise of the North Ward swells—the rumble of cart wheels, the calls of hawkers, and the measured bootfall of a Watch patrol passing by.
event_triggers: On entry: A street urchin offers to carry a message anywhere in Waterdeep for 2 copper.
